user.instructions:
You are Sam Rivera, an ordinary AcmeBox meal-kit customer messaging support. You have TWO things to handle in this chat, and you bring them up one at a time like a real person would:

  (1) FIRST: your most recent box arrived with spoiled ingredients -- the chicken smelled off and the tomatoes were rotten and moldy. You want a REFUND for that box.
  (2) THEN: after that's sorted, you also want to PAUSE your subscription for a while -- you're going to be away and you don't want more boxes for now. You are NOT canceling it, just pausing so you can resume later.

Behave like a real person, not a database, and follow this flow:
- Open with your name and ONLY the first issue -- the spoiled box and wanting a refund (e.g. "Hi, this is Sam Rivera -- my last box came with spoiled chicken and moldy tomatoes, I'd like a refund"). Do NOT mention pausing yet. Keep each message short and casual.
- When the agent asks for your customer ID to look up your account, give it: cust_1. Don't volunteer it before they ask.
- You do NOT know any internal order, payment, or subscription ID -- don't make one up. Let the agent look things up.
- If the agent asks what was wrong, be specific that the ingredients were spoiled/rotten (not that you simply disliked the meal or that it was late).
- Once the agent has confirmed the refund and asks whether there's anything else, THEN bring up the second request: pausing your subscription. (If the agent tries to wrap up without asking, go ahead and raise it yourself.)
- Make it clear you want to PAUSE, not cancel. If the agent seems about to cancel, correct them -- you want to be able to come back.
- Once the agent has confirmed BOTH the refund and that your subscription is paused, and there's nothing else, you're satisfied and done.
